rules.title: Use SELECT DISTINCT in nearly every PROC SQL query
rules.body: Unless there is a deliberate documented reason not to. This surfaces unintended fan-out early — if row count > distinct ID count, something is causing duplication and we can investigate before continuing.
rules.tip: Add the commented-out row count from the log next to the code that created the table. This lets validators confirm they are on the same track. Not needed for every table, but definitely at key tables.

rules.title: Pull with inline subquery, then join for additional fields
rules.body: Pull claims using an inline subquery in the WHERE clause first, then join for additional fields afterward. This avoids inefficient claim pulls. Also use the &med_varis. macro to select only needed variables — never pull entire claim lines.

rules.title: NULLs in a NOT IN list cause all rows to silently fall through
rules.body: If the subquery or list passed to NOT IN contains any NULL values, SQL returns zero rows — all records fail the condition. This is silent and produces no error. Use COALESCE or restructure as a LEFT JOIN with WHERE key IS NULL.

rules.title: AND binds more tightly than OR — always use explicit parentheses
rules.body: In complex CASE WHEN and WHERE conditions, AND has higher precedence than OR. Relying on implicit precedence produces wrong results that are hard to spot. This is a recurring source of classification bugs.

rules.title: Date filters on joined tables belong in ON, not WHERE
rules.body: Putting a right-table date filter in the WHERE clause eliminates rows where the join found no match — exactly the rows a LEFT JOIN is meant to preserve. A LEFT JOIN with a WHERE filter on the right-side table silently becomes an INNER JOIN.

rules.title: Always use UNION ALL when stacking yearly tables
rules.body: UNION silently removes duplicate rows. When stacking yearly claims tables, legitimate duplicate records (same member, same service, different year) will be dropped without warning. Use UNION ALL to preserve all rows.

rules.title: CALCULATED aliases for complex CASE WHEN are unreliable in WHERE
rules.body: Refactor into a subquery wrapper and filter on the outer layer instead of referencing the CALCULATED alias directly.

subsections.title: OVER PARTITION BY — Not in SAS 9.4

rules.title: Window functions are unavailable in SAS 9.4 PROC SQL
rules.body: OVER / PARTITION BY do not exist in SAS 9.4 PROC SQL. Replace with a join to a pre-built year-total subquery.
